entry.title: Вебінар "Ринок SAP фахівців України 2026". Дата: 15 квітня, 18.30 за Києвом
entry.content:
15 квітня, о 18.30 за київським часом:
Розкажемо про SAP вакансії та виклики найму SAP фахівців, зарплати та рейти фрилансерів, конкуренцію з ринком ЄС, розвиток SAP ком’юніті в Україні, програми підтримки ринку фахівців з боку вендору, консалтингових партнерів та багато іншої інформації.

 Реєстрація до участі в вебінарі 

🟦 Кому буде корисно:
- Менеджерам з найму, HR спеціалістам, IT/SAP рекрутерам (in-house та агенції).
- SAP фахівцям та менеджерам, які відповідають за забезпечення команд SAP спеціалістами.
- Всім, хто хоче бути в темі ринку SAP в 2026 році.

🟦 Дата: 15.04.2026, середа
Час: 18:30 (за київським часом)
Тривалість: 60 хвилин
Реєстрація до участі в вебінарі 

Спікери:

➡️ Олена Муренець

SAP Recruitment Expert/SAP Resource Manager. CORALI Business Resource (SAP Recruitment/SAP Staffing)/Україна, Польща та
країни ЄС. 15 років роботи в різних країнах на боці рекрутингових агенцій, SAP консалтингових компаній, кінцевих замовників SAP рішень

➡️ Юлія Фіцак 

ІТ рекрутерка з 9+ роками досвіду. Працювала з ринками України та Європи. 6+ років у підборі SAP спеціалістів. Кар’єрна консультантка та менторка.

--
Поговоримо про наш SAP ринок? 😀


#SAP #SAPUkraine #SAPJobsUkraine #SAPRecruiter #SAPWebinar

entry.title: Somos SAP Community Curitiba – Luiz Souza
entry.content:
Este post faz parte da série “Somos SAP Community Curitiba”, criada para compartilhar as histórias das pessoas que ajudam a construir e fortalecer o ecossistema SAP na nossa cidade.

A ideia da série foi apresentada neste post de abertura:
  Introdução - Série da comunidade: Somos SAP Community Curitiba

A proposta é simples: responder algumas perguntas e contar um pouco da nossa jornada dentro da comunidade.

😎Quem sou eu na SAP Community Curitiba?
Sou Luiz Souza, Sou desenvolvedor raiz desde 2003 e atualmente focado em sap invovação e tecnologia e Clean Core. E também sou curador da SAP Community Curitiba, atuando na organização e condução de iniciativas que conectam profissionais, promovem aprendizado contínuo e fortalecem o ecossistema SAP local. Tenho um papel ativo na criação de experiências como eventos, encontros técnicos, sempre buscando gerar valor real para a comunidade.

🤔O que mais me motiva a continuar fazendo parte da SAP Community Curitiba?
O que mais me motiva é o impacto direto nas pessoas. Ver profissionais evoluindo, compartilhando conhecimento e se conectando através da comunidade é algo extremamente gratificante. Além disso, a troca constante com especialistas e a oportunidade de estar próximo das inovações da SAP, como BTP, Clean Core e IA, me impulsionam a continuar contribuindo.

💎Como fazer parte da comunidade impactou minha jornada profissional ou pessoal?
Fazer parte da comunidade teve um impacto significativo na minha jornada. Profissionalmente, fortaleceu minha posição como nos temas como SAP BTP, extensão e integração, além de ampliar meu networking com profissionais do Brasil e do exterior. Pessoalmente, desenvolvi habilidades de comunicação e confiança — especialmente ao conduzir eventos, inclusive em inglês — e vivi experiências marcantes que elevaram meu nível como profissional e como pessoa.

🤠Que conselho você daria para alguém que está começando agora no ecossistema SAP e ainda não conhece a comunidade? Não desista dos seus sonhos, e se mantenha sempre desafiado!

📸Um momento especial na comunidade

Foram vários, mas vou deixar uma trajetória para dizer que vale cada esforço!

SAP Inside Track São Paulo - 2023 - Como participante


SAP CWB Xperience 2025 - Ministrando Hands-on


SAP Inside Track Curitiba 2025 - Como Apoiador e Palestrante!




Recentemente como Host de um  SAP Code Jam 2026 mais que especial, com figuras internacionais!


🌐Conecte-se comigo
LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/luiz-carlos-de-souza-ab6a2212/ 

✨Convidados para continuar a série
Para continuar a série Somos SAP Community Curitiba, convido: @MD_MichaelDouglas  @Lpinheiro   @Babbingtonn  @denisjunior  para compartilharem suas histórias e experiências na comunidade.

Além das perguntas da série:
1️⃣Quem sou eu na SAP Community Curitiba?
2️⃣O que mais me motiva a continuar fazendo parte da SAP Community Curitiba?
3️⃣Como fazer parte da comunidade impactou minha jornada profissional ou pessoal?

Deixo também uma pergunta adicional:
❓ Como você se você vê na comunidade daqui 2 anos ?

✨Frase final
 Entrar na SAP Community é mais do que aprender tecnologia — é se conectar com pessoas que vão acelerar sua evolução, abrir portas e transformar sua carreira de dentro para fora.

#SomosSAPCommunityCWB
#SAPCommunityCWB
#SAPCommunity

entry.title: SAP Inside Track Melbourne - 19th Mar 2026
entry.content:
Thank you to the SAP Community members who came out in force to support the first SAP Inside Track event for 2026 on 19th Mar.

We would like to thank our friends at AWS for hosting us and providing us drinks and to our new book sponsors Espresso-tutorials everyone who registered their interest to come the march event got access to a Espresso-tutorials SAP learning platform

 

We had an awesome presentation on CAP Architectural considerations by Ben Patterson followed by discussion around the technical challenges we are facing in our day to day projects.

 

Looking forward to the next SIT on 20th May 26 as we have two presentation about SAP Joule Enablement for SAP Ariba and Authorisations in BTP apps

 

 

Would like to finally thank our sponsors once again @SAPAmazon and @EspressoTutorialsGmbH for their support

 

See you on 20th May

 

https://community.sap.com/t5/melbourne-events/sap-inside-track-melbourne-20th-may-2026/ev-p/14345378

 

 

Cheers,

SAP Community -Melbourne

@pjcools, @Machillanda @BenPatterson  @Sai_Sriram @davidnye

entry.title: Event-Driven Integration Simplified: Why Salesforce Pub/Sub Adapter Is the Right Choice in SAP CI
entry.content:
Introduction 

If you are building integrations between Salesforce and SAP using SAP Cloud Integration (SAP CI), the Salesforce Adapter has likely been your trusted workhorse, and it should remain so. It handles CRUD operations beautifully: querying records with SOQL, creating leads, updating opportunities, upserting accounts with External IDs. 

But here is the question this blog is really asking: when your integration need is event-driven, are you using the right tool? 

Many customers today subscribe to Salesforce Platform Events or Change Data Capture (CDC) events through the Salesforce Adapter's Streaming API capability. It works, but it relies on the CometD protocol, a technology that was designed for a different era of web communication. SAP CI now offers a dedicated Salesforce Pub/Sub Adapter, built on the modern gRPC protocol, that is a significantly better fit for event-based integration. 

This blog is not about replacing the Salesforce Adapter. It is about using the right adapter for the right job. For CRUD operations, keep using the Salesforce Adapter. For events, it is time to move to the Pub/Sub Adapter. 

The Salesforce Adapter:  

The Salesforce Adapter in SAP CI is a full-featured integration adapter that supports: 

  • REST API : Standard CRUD operations on Salesforce objects 
  • REST API Composite : Batch multiple operations in a single call 
  • Bulk API : High-volume data loads 
  • APEX REST : Invoke custom server-side Salesforce logic 
  • Streaming API : Subscribe to real-time event channels 

That last capability “Streaming API” is where things get interesting. The Salesforce Adapter does support event-based communication, and many customers are using it today for Platform Events and PushTopics. This is not a broken integration. It works. 

However, underneath the Streaming API is the CometD protocol, CometD is an open-source messaging bus that implements the Bayeux protocol (a specification for server-push communication over HTTP). Salesforce's Streaming API is built on top of CometD.

The Pub/Sub Adapter: Built for Events from the Ground Up 

SAP CI's Salesforce Pub/Sub Sender Adapter takes a fundamentally different approach. Instead of the Streaming API's CometD layer, it uses Salesforce's Pub/Sub API, which is built on gRPC over HTTP/2. 

This is not just a protocol swap, it is a rethinking of how event subscriptions should work in an enterprise integration context. 

The difference gRPC protocol brings: 

gRPC is a modern, high-performance Remote Procedure Call framework developed by Google. It operates over HTTP/2, which enables: 

  • Persistent bidirectional streams : a single connection stays open and allows messages to flow in both directions simultaneously, without the need for repeated handshakes 
  • Binary serialisation with Protocol Buffers (protobuf) : event payloads are compact and efficient, making gRPC approximately 7–10x faster than REST in payload processing 
  • Multiplexing and Low Latency : multiple streams over a single connection, with no head-of-line blocking and binary message serialization through Protocol Buffers, resulting in lower latency and faster data delivery. 
  • Native flow control : built into the HTTP/2 layer, giving both sides control over how much data is in flight 

In practical terms: where CometD is a series of HTTP/1.1 conversations imitating a stream, gRPC is a genuine, persistent, bidirectional channel. 

A Side-by-Side Comparison: Streaming API (CometD) vs Pub/Sub API (gRPC) 

Capability 

Streaming API via Salesforce Adapter 

Pub/Sub API via Pub/Sub Adapter 

Underlying Protocol 

CometD / Bayeux over HTTP/1.1 

gRPC over HTTP/2 

Connection Type 

Long-polling (simulated stream) 

Genuine persistent bidirectional stream 

Flow Control 

None : server pushes at its own pace 

Pull-based : client controls event intake 

Payload Format 

JSON or XML 

Binary (Apache Avro / Protobuf) 

Performance 

Moderate 

High ~7–10x faster payload processing 

Invalid ReplayID Support   

Not available  

Available , Latest and Earliest events 

Duplicate Handling 

Manual implementation needed 

Built into the SAP CI adapter 

Authentication 

OAuth 2.0 and OAuth 2.0 JWT 

OAuth 2.0 and OAuth 2.0 JWT 

Event Types Supported 

Platform Events, PushTopics, CDC 

Platform Events, CDC, Event Monitoring 

 

The Right Adapter for the Right Job 

Here is how to think about it: 

Integration Need 

Recommended Adapter 

Query Salesforce records (SOQL) 

 Salesforce Adapter 

Create or update Salesforce records 

 Salesforce Adapter 

Upsert with External ID 

 Salesforce Adapter 

Bulk data load 

 Salesforce Adapter (Bulk API) 

Subscribe to Platform Events in real time 

 Salesforce Pub/Sub Adapter 

Subscribe to Change Data Capture events 

 Salesforce Pub/Sub Adapter 

Subscribe to Event Monitoring events 

 Salesforce Pub/Sub Adapter 

Publish events  

 Salesforce Pub/Sub Adapter 

Think of them as complementary adapters operating in different lanes. In a sophisticated Salesforce–SAP integration landscape, you will likely use both the Salesforce Adapter handling your data operations, and the Pub/Sub Adapter handling your event subscriptions. 

Migration Considerations 

The perception vs. The reality 

One of the most common reactions when consultants first propose this migration is concern about scope. The assumption is that moving to a new event protocol means coordinating with the Salesforce team, changing Platform Event definitions and managing a coordinated cutover. It feels like a project that needs multiple teams and a change freeze window. 

That concern is understandable, but it is not accurate. 

There are zero changes required on the Salesforce side. Your Platform Event definitions stay exactly as they are. The CDC configuration stays exactly as it is. The Pub/Sub Adapter is just a new subscriber, using a different transport underneath.  

The Salesforce team does not need to be involved at all. The configuration experience in SAP CI also remains largely familiar. The Salesforce Pub/Sub Adapter shares most of its configuration fields with the standard Salesforce Adapter, with only a few additional parameters introduced specifically for event-driven communication.  

In the section below, we will focus only on these new fields, their configuration, and their significance. 

Step 1 : Adapter Configuration: 

In SAP CI, create a new Integration Flow in your package. Add a Sender participant, connect it to the Start Message event, and select Salesforce Pub/Sub from the adapter dropdown. 

Connection Tab:

Parameter Description: 

Host 

(New field) 

Specify the host to which the proxy requests are sent for the gRPC API (towards the gRPC host). The proxy requests to the gRPC host will go through the proxy. 

Example: api.pubsub.salesforce.com 

Port 

(New field) 

Specify the port to which the proxy requests are sent for the gRPC API (towards the gRPC port). The proxy requests to the gRPC port will go through the proxy. 

Example: 7443 

Tenant ID 

(New field) 

 

Specify the Salesforce Organization ID, this can be found in Company Information within the "Setup" page on Salesforce 

Authentication 

Select the authentication type to be used for the connection to Salesforce: 

  • OAuth 2.0 Username-Password 
  • OAuth 2.0 JWT Bearer 

Connection Check Interval (in ms) 

(New field) 

 

Specify the interval in milliseconds to verify the connection validity of the gRPC. 

 

Processing Tab: 

Channel Name :Enter the channel name 
Replay ID Approach : 

Replay ID is the unique ID that is generate for every event, The adapter offers 3 approaches here. 

  • Events from a Specific Position (Custom): 
    Use this option to listen to events starting from a specific Replay ID. Ideal when you want to resume from a known checkpoint. Once selected, a parameter called Replay ID is enabled where ID can be specified. 
  • Events from the Earliest Position (Earliest): 
    This option starts listening from the oldest available event in the event bus at the time of adapter deployment. It captures all events retained in the event bus 
  • Events from the Latest Position (Latest): 
    This option starts listening from the latest event at the moment the adapter is deployed. It ignores older events and captures only new incoming ones from that point onward. 
Request Batch Size (New field)Specify the number of events to be requested in the Salesforce fetchRequest. The Maximum events that Salesforce can return in a single call is 100 
Replay Preset for Invalid Replay ID (New field) 

Select the replay option in case sfdc.platform.eventbus.grpc.subscription.fetch.replayid.corrupted error occurs: 

  • Events from latest position (Latest) 
  • Events from earliest position (Earliest) 
Duplicate Check Expiration (in ms) (New field): SAP CI operates with two active worker nodes that can simultaneously consume events from Salesforce, which introduces a possibility of duplicate processing. The adapter handles this internally by performing a duplicate check. This parameter defines the expiration time (in milliseconds) for which an event is considered during the duplicate check, helping to prevent redundant processing. 
Process Errors as an Event (New field) : 
This option is useful for debugging errors occurring on the Salesforce backend. When enabled, errors are streamed as events and can be received in SAP CI, allowing you to monitor and track issues directly within your integration flow. If disabled, connection or processing errors related to event streams will be logged only in Salesforce, without being sent to SAP CI.

Step 2 : Handle the payload format

While both adapters ultimately deliver the same business information (for example, Platform Events or Change Data Capture events), the way this data is structured and delivered differs slightly.

Key Differences 

  • Payload Format:
    • Salesforce Adapter delivers payloads directly in XML format  
    • Pub/Sub Adapter delivers payloads in Avro (binary), which SAP CI internally deserializes into a JSON-like structure  
    • This requires an additional JSON-to-XML conversion step in the iFlow
  • Structure & Envelope:
    • Salesforce Adapter wraps the payload inside a structured envelope like:
      • <subscribeTopic> → <data> → <payload> → <event>
    • Pub/Sub Adapter provides a flatter and cleaner structure, with:  
      • <payload> containing all fields  
      • Metadata fields like schemaId and id at root level
  • Field Representation:
    • In Pub/Sub payloads, all fields of the entity are typically present, even if empty
    • In Streaming API, the payload is often more compact and selective  
  • ChangeEventHeader Node:
    • Present in both formats and largely consistent
    • Pub/Sub provides additional elements like:  
      • nulledFields  
      • diffFields 
    • This gives more flexibility for advanced event processing 

Salesforce Adapter (Streaming API) Response: 

Salesforce Pub Sub Adapter Response: 

Only the following adjustments are needed in SAP CI: 

  1. Add a JSON-to-XML Converter  
    1. Immediately after the Pub/Sub Sender adapter  
    2. This aligns the payload with your existing XML-based mappings
  2. Minor Mapping Adjustments (if required) 
    1. Update XPath references if your existing mapping depends on:  
      • Envelope structure (/subscribeTopic/data/...) 
    2. With Pub/Sub, mapping typically starts directly from /root/payload/...  
  3. Leverage Additional Metadata (Optional)  
    1. Fields like nulledFields and diffFields can enhance logic  
    2. Not mandatory for migration, but useful for optimization 

In most cases, existing mappings can be reused with only minor adjustments if required, as the core business data and ChangeEventHeader structure remain consistent across both adapters. 

Step 3 : Test in parallel, then switch 

This is where the migration story becomes genuinely low-risk. Because the Pub/Sub Adapter is simply an additional subscriber on the same event channel, you can run both your existing Salesforce Adapter iFlow and your new Pub/Sub Adapter iFlow at the same time. Salesforce will deliver the event to both consumers independently. 

This means the migration path looks like this: 

  • Deploy the new Pub/Sub iFlow alongside the existing Salesforce Adapter iFlow, both consume the same Platform Event or CDC channel 
  • Test in parallel, validate payload processing, replay behaviour, error handling, and downstream integration in the new iFlow while the old one continues running in production 
  • Switch over when you are confident, decommission the old Streaming API iFlow on your own timeline 

There is no big-bang cutover. There is no risk window where your production integration is down. The old iFlow keeps running until you decide to retire it. This is as safe a migration as it gets. 

What stays the same, what changes 

 

Stays the same 

Changes 

Salesforce side 

Platform Events, Apex triggers, CDC config, channel names 

Nothing 

SAP CI Auth 

Same Connected App, same credential alias 

Nothing 

SAP CI iFlow 

Downstream calls, error handling 

New sender adapter, Avro → XML conversion step,  Mapping (Minor changes)

 The Salesforce team does not need a ticket. The project does not need a steering committee. It needs a developer, a new iFlow, and a test run. 

Conclusion 

The Salesforce Adapter’s Streaming API capability has served its purpose well. But at its core, CometD remains a long-polling workaround from a pre-HTTP/2 era. 

The Salesforce Pub/Sub Adapter is the modern foundation for event-driven integration in SAP CI, offering better performance, reliability, and control through persistent connections, flow control, and built-in deduplication. 

Use each tool for what it does best: 

  • Salesforce Adapter for CRUD, bulk operations, and queries  
  • Pub/Sub Adapter for real-time, event-driven integration  

The tools are already in your hands. Now it’s about using them the right way. 

References 

  • SAP Help: Configure Salesforce Pub/Sub Sender Adapter 
  • Real-Time Salesforce Integration with SAP CI: Introducing Salesforce Pub/Sub Adapter 
  • SAP API Business Hub: Salesforce Pub/Sub Adapter Package 

Hope this helps!

Cheers,

Punith

entry.title: Відкриті SAP вебінари: Manufacturing & Supply Chain
entry.content:
Квітень-травень — насичений сезон подій від SAP у сфері виробництва та Supply Chain! 🏭Реальні кейси клієнтів, живі демо та глибокі технічні інсайти — все це у трьох відкритих онлайн заходах.

Обирайте те, що актуально для вас, та реєструйтесь.

  • 21.04.2026, 15:00-16:00 (Kyiv time)
    DM, EWM Customer Talk with Centillion 
    🏢Індустрія: Life Sciences  📊 Сегмент: Medium Enterprise 🌐 Мова заходу: Англійська
    📢Спікери:
    Dimitar Lengerov -  Керівник відділу цифрових технологій
    Antoaneta Levova - Менеджер проєкту
    Kristian Markov - Керівник відділу інженерів виробничих процесів

    SAP Customer Conversations - це формат, де реальний клієнт SAP ділиться досвідом впровадження: ключовими рішеннями, викликами та досягнутими бізнес-результатами. Після презентації — LIVE дискусія та інтерактивна сесія Q&A.
    Centillion Ltd. — інноваційний болгарський виробник електронних пристроїв та систем для медицини, авіоніки та промислового обладнання. Компанія працює на сучасному екологічному виробництві площею 7 800 м².

    На заході команда поділиться досвідом впровадження RISE with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, SAP Extended Warehouse Management та SAP Digital Manufacturing — як ці рішення допомогли модернізувати бізнес-процеси, забезпечити аналітику у реальному часі, контроль якості та наскрізну цифровізацію виробництва сьогодні і в перспективі.

    Зареєструватися

  • 16.04 / 14.05 / 11.06, 20:00 – 21:00 (Kyiv time)
    Manufacturing - make to stock. MES solution
    Виробничі затримки, ручне планування та непередбачувані збої в ланцюжку постачань?

    Приєднуйтесь повторюваноно вебінару, задля живої демонстрації та дізнайтеся, як провідні виробники автоматизують процеси від планування до виконання замовлень за допомогою SAP Cloud ERP та MES.
    На вас чекає: Автоматизація MRP та виробничих замовлень, динамічне планування та оптимізація операцій за допомогою AI.

    Кількість місць обмежена.
    Зареєструватися

  • 28.04, 30.04, 05.05, 07.05, 16:00 - 17:00 (Kyiv time)
    Digital Manufacturing webinar series

    Серія вебінарів про управління тестуванням та автоматизацію для SAP Digital Manufacturing — від основ до масштабування.
    28.04 — Огляд можливостей Test Management у SAP Cloud ALM для SAP Digital Manufacturing: прозорість, відстежуваність та якість між бізнес- і ІТ-командами. Практичні приклади від партнера Сoncircle.
    30.04 — Інтеграція SAP Cloud ALM з SAP Test Automation by Tricentis: як поєднати управління тестами з автоматизацією для наскрізного забезпечення якості.
    05.05 — Повний end-to-end сценарій автоматизації тестування від Сoncircle: управління процесами, ризиками та квартальними релізами SAP Digital Manufacturing на реальних прикладах.
    07.05 — Поглиблений рівень: масштабована архітектура тест-автоматизації, шаблони, управління тестовими даними та best practices для Tosca Cloud.

    Зареєструватися

Долучайтесь, діліться з колегами, клієнтами та партнерами — ці події відкриті для всіх!

entry.title: Hello SAP community my name is Vishwa Bharadhwaj S P I'm excited to start my journey as a student
entry.content:
Hello SAP community, my name is Vishwa Bharadhwaj S P. I’m currently a student, and over time I’ve been exploring my interest in technology and trying to understand how things work beyond just what’s taught in class. My journey as a student hasn’t been perfect, but it has helped me realize the importance of learning on my own and improving step by step.  I spend time building my knowledge through different resources and trying to get better at what I do. I’m still figuring things out, but I’m focused on growing and building a strong career over time.

Here are the five questions I would like to answer as a a part of the "Hello SAP Community" initiative 

1. What is your professional life dream?

I want to build a career in technology where I’m actually useful, not just doing tasks for the sake of it. I’ve always been curious about how systems work, so I want to be in a place where I can solve real problems and see the impact of what I’m doing. I don’t have everything figured out yet, but I know I want to keep learning, get better at what I do, and eventually reach a level where people trust my work.

2. Where do you see yourself in 5 years?

If I’m being honest, I don’t have a perfect picture. But I do see myself more confident and experienced than I am now. I want to be working on real projects, not just learning basics, and actually understanding what I’m doing instead of guessing. I’d like to be in a role where I’m improving consistently and not feeling stuck.

3. What is your hobby?

Most of the time, I end up exploring new technologies or learning something online. It’s not always planned, I just go down random topics and try to understand how things work. I also play chess quite a bit. It helps me slow down and think properly instead of rushing decisions. Sometimes I lose badly, but that’s part of it. Going to the gym is another thing I’ve been consistent with. It clears my head. It’s one of the few places where progress feels straightforward, you show up, you work, and over time you see change.

4. Which 4 things do you absolutely want to achieve in life?

I want to build a career where I feel stable and know what I’m doing.
I want to keep learning instead of becoming comfortable and stagnant.
I want to do something useful with what I learn, not just work for a salary.
I want to reach a point where my journey makes sense to someone else who’s trying to figure things out.

5. What is the best lesson your parents taught you?

They didn’t really sit me down and explain big life lessons, but I’ve seen how they handled things. They kept going even when things weren’t easy. That stuck with me. So for me, the lesson is simple, keep doing the work even when you don’t feel like it. Don’t depend too much on motivation. Just stay consistent and things slowly start making sense.

6.How do you choose which area to focus on in SAP as a student?

 I think it depends on your background. Since I come from mechanical engineering, I find SAP Material Management more relatable because it connects with materials, inventory, and supply processes that I’ve already studied in theory. SAP MM feels like digital side of the same process where everything is organized, tracked, planned and optimized

My SAP Inside Track 2026 Experience 

Attended SAP Inside Track 2026 with my friends, and honestly that made the experience better. We discussed a lot after each session, and it helped me understand things more clearly instead of just listening and moving on. I had great insights on Gen Ai and Applied research and the clarity of the content was perfect.

This was inside the SAP Labs Innovation Park. Seeing the model and how everything is structured gave me a better idea of how large-scale systems and environments are planned. It felt more practical than just learning concepts in theory.

The campus itself left an impression. It did not feel like a typical place, it felt like a space where actual innovation happens. Being there made things feel more real and gave me a better sense of where I want to reach.

Now let's hear it from you.

One thing I'm still thinking about after the session is

How are AI agents being practically integrated into enterprise systems, and what challenges come up during implementation?

entry.title: Hello SAP Community My Name Is Nidhi N H and I'm excited to begin my jorney here
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community!
My name is Nidhi N H, a Mechanical Engineering student at REVA University in Bangalore, India.

 Our SAP journey started as something completely new for us. At first, we didn’t have much idea about what SAP actually is or how it works. It felt confusing because it was different from what we were used to learning. But slowly, our curiosity increased, and we decided to explore it step by step.

I am very happy to be part of the Hello SAP Community initiative. This opportunity allows beginners like me to explore the SAP ecosystem and connect with professionals around the world.
As a student, I am interested in learning new technologies and understanding how SAP solutions help businesses manage their operations efficiently. Through the SAP Community, I hope to learn from experts, discover new ideas, and grow my professional network.

I look forward to sharing my learning journey and contributing to the SAP Community in the future.

 

Thank you SAP Community for this wonderful opportunity!

Here are some exciting questions I've chosen to answer as a part of the Hello SAP Community. initiative;

1.what is your professional life dream ?

My professional dream is to build a successful career in technology and engineering. I want to continuously learn new skills and work on innovative solutions that help organizations improve their efficiency. I also aim to contribute to the tech community and grow as a professional in the global technology ecosystem.

2.where do you see yourself in 5 years ?

In the next five years, I see myself as a skilled professional working in the technology industry. I hope to gain strong knowledge, practical experience, and work on real-world projects. I also want to keep learning and improving myself while contributing positively to my organization and community.

3.what is your hobby ?

One of my hobbies is learning about new technologies and exploring new ideas related to engineering and innovation. I also enjoy reading, learning new skills online, and improving my knowledge through different educational platforms.

4. which 5 thing do you absolutely want to achives in life ?

There are several goals I want to achieve in my life:

Build a successful professional career.
Continuously learn and develop new skills.
Contribute to society through knowledge and innovation.
Inspire others through my journey and experiences.

5.what is the best lesson your parents taught you ?

The best lesson my parents taught me is the importance of hard work, honesty, and patience. They always encouraged me to stay focused on my goals and never give up during difficult situations. These values continue to guide me in my education and future career.

6. How do you see yourself helping society with SAP knowledge?
With SAP knowledge, I aim to contribute by improving efficiency in industries such as manufacturing and agriculture. By using ERP systems, I can help organizations optimize resources, reduce waste, and improve productivity. This indirectly benefits society by supporting sustainable development and better resource management.

With the knowledge I gain, I aim to develop practical and affordable solutions for real-world problems. For example, I want to work on smart agriculture systems like weed detection and automated irrigation to support farmers. I also hope to contribute to sustainable technologies that reduce environmental impact. By applying my skills, I want to make technology more accessible and useful, especially for rural communities.

7. How will SAP help your career?
SAP will enhance my profile by adding in-demand skills and give me exposure to enterprise-level systems, which are highly valued in the job market.

8. How will you apply SAP knowledge in your field?
As a mechanical engineering student, I can use SAP in areas like production planning, supply chain management, and inventory control to improve efficiency in manufacturing industries.

 

My Experience at SAP TechEd Bangalore.

Recently, I had the opportunity to attend SAP TechEd Bangalore, and it was an amazing learning experience for me. As a student interested in technology and innovation, being part of such a global tech event was truly inspiring. One of the most exciting parts of the event was exploring the Demo Pods, where SAP experts explained different technologies and solutions. It was great to see how SAP technologies like BTP are used to solve real-world business problems.

Key Takeaways from the event.
Generative AI is essential now – Tools like SAP Joule show that AI isn’t just an add-on anymore; it’s becoming the core of productivity and automation.
Data drives sustainability – Using BTP for ESG reporting shows how data can turn sustainability goals into real, measurable impact.
Across every theme at TechEd, Al, automation, integration, and developer enablement, one thing stood out: data readiness is the true enabler of transformation. Without it, even the most advanced tools can't deliver their full value.

I had the wonderful opportunity to attend SAP TechEd Bangalore along with friends from my college. It was a great experience for all of us to be part of such a global technology event. Our college group explored different sessions, demo pods, and interacted with SAP experts. We also had the chance to connect with members of the SAP Community and learn about new technologies like SAP BTP, AI, and cloud innovations.


Now, I would like to finish the post by asking you all a question which you're invited to answer in the comments section:
What is a "mind-blowing" and fascinating technology you expect to be mainstream by 2030??
“I’d love to hear your voice in this conversation—share your ideas, insights, or predictions in the comments. Your perspectives will enrich this dialogue, and I’m genuinely excited to learn from the thoughts you bring to the table.”

entry.title: Hello SAP Community! 👋 – I’m Sanjay, and This Is My Journey
entry.content:
My name is Sanjay M N, and I’m currently pursuing Mechanical Engineering at REVA University in Bangalore, India.

I’m truly excited to be part of the Hello SAP Community initiative. For a beginner like me, this is a great opportunity to explore the SAP ecosystem and connect with professionals across the world.

As a student from a core engineering background, I’ve always been curious about how technology connects with real-world industries. This curiosity is what led me to explore SAP and understand how it helps organizations manage large-scale operations efficiently.

I’m also excited to share my learning journey and contribute back to the SAP Community as I grow.

Thank you, SAP Community, for this wonderful opportunity! 🙌

🌱A Little About Me

1. What is your professional life dream?

My professional dream is to build a stable and meaningful career. I have a strong interest in railways, and I’m planning to prepare for the RRB exam to become a Junior Engineer. At the same time, I’m also exploring technology like SAP because I want to keep learning and stay open to new opportunities. I’m curious to understand how systems like SAP are used behind the scenes to manage large-scale operations like railways.

2. Where do you see yourself in 5 years?

In 5 years, I see myself in a responsible position, hopefully working in the railways as a Junior Engineer or in a role where I can apply my mechanical knowledge. I want to be someone who is confident in their skills and keeps improving with time.

3. What is your hobby?

I enjoy activities like swimming, cycling, and reading books. These help me relax and stay balanced. I also like exploring new things and learning online whenever I get time.

4. What is the best lesson your parents taught you?

One thing my parents always taught me is to stay strong during tough times and not lose hope. No matter how difficult the situation is, they believe that if we stay patient and keep putting in effort, things will eventually get better. This mindset has really shaped how I handle challenges.

5. What do you imagine is the future of your topic?

I feel the future will be a combination of engineering and technologies like AI and cloud. Even in industries like railways and manufacturing, systems will become smarter with automation and data. I believe platforms like SAP will play an important role in managing and improving these systems, and I’m interested in learning more about it.

🚀My Experience at SAP TechEd Bangalore 2025

Recently, I had the opportunity to attend SAP TechEd Bangalore, and it was truly an inspiring experience. As a student, being part of such a global tech event gave me a completely new perspective on how technology is shaping industries.

One of the most exciting parts was exploring the Demo Pods, where SAP experts demonstrated how technologies like SAP BTP are used to solve real-world business challenges.

🌟A Memorable Interaction

One of the most inspiring moments for me was interacting with Moshe Naveh during the SAP EdTech Tour 2025.

He explained how the SAP Community works and how it connects people from different parts of the world. What really stayed with me was his advice—that by being active in this community, we can interact with industry professionals, learn from their experiences, and grow together.

As a beginner, that conversation was truly motivating. It made me realize that learning is not just about courses or content, but also about engaging with the right community.

🚀 My Learning & Experiences

 

Other than SAP TechEd Bangalore event I also had the chance to attend the SAP Insight Track (SIT) at the SAP Labs India Innovation Park, Devanahalli. It was another great experience where I attended multiple sessions and got exposed to different ideas and technologies within SAP.

Along with this, I recently completed a certification course on SAP Procurement Process Overview, which helped me understand the basics of how procurement works in SAP systems.

🤝The Best Part: The Community

While the sessions and technologies were impressive, the most valuable part of the experience was the people.

Connecting with SAP experts, interacting with community members, and attending the event with my college friends made the experience even more meaningful. These interactions are where real learning happens.

💬 Let’s Connect

I would love to hear your thoughts on this & learn from this community...

👉“As a Mechanical Engineering student, how can I align my background with SAP, especially with the growing role of AI in industries?”
👉“Also, how can I make the most out of the SAP Community to accelerate my learning?”

This is just the beginning of my journey, and I’m excited to learn, connect, and grow with this amazing community.

entry.title: Hello SAP community! My name is B C NIKHIL and I'm excited to start my journey.
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community

My name is B C NIKHIL and I’m currently pursuing Mechanical Engineering at REVA University in Bangalore, India.

I am very happy to be part of the Hello SAP Community initiative. This opportunity allows beginners like me to explore the SAP ecosystem and connect with professionals around the world.

As a student, I am interested in learning new technologies and understanding how SAP solutions help businesses manage their operations efficiently. Through the SAP Community, I hope to learn from experts, discover new ideas, and grow my professional network.

I look forward to sharing my learning journey and contributing to the  SAP  Community in the future.

Thank you SAP Community for this wonderful opportunity!

Here are the questions I chose to answer as a part of the initiative:

1. Where do you see yourself in 5 years?

In five years, I envision myself as a Senior Thermal Design Engineer or a CFD Specialist. Having transitioned from my studies at REVA University into the professional world, I see myself leading complex simulations for high-performance thermal systems perhaps in the electric vehicle (EV) sector or aerospace—ensuring that cutting-edge technology runs efficiently and safely.

2. What is your hobby?

One of my favorite ways to spend time outside of engineering is following IPL cricket, specifically cheering for Royal Challengers Bangalore (RCB). There is a certain "engineering" to the game the strategy, the physics of a fast bowl, and the data analytics involved that makes it incredibly engaging to watch. It’s the perfect high-energy balance to the focus required for academic research.

3. If you could be a superhero, who would you be? What do you enjoy most in your studies and why?

I’d choose Iron Man (Tony Stark). Beyond the suit, I admire the way he uses engineering to solve seemingly impossible global problems. In my own studies, what I enjoy most is Computational Fluid Dynamics (CFD). There is something fascinating about taking invisible forces, like heat and airflow, and using math and software to make them visible and predictable. It feels like having a "superpower" to see how the physical world works at a molecular level.

4. What do you imagine is the future of your topic?

The future of Thermal Sciences and Mechanical Design lies in the "Digital Twin" revolution. I predict we will move toward a world where every physical machine from a tiny drone motor to a massive power plant has a real-time AI counterpart. We won't just design a part and hope it works; we will use generative design and AI-driven fluid analysis to create organic, ultra-efficient shapes that humans could never manually draft, potentially reducing energy waste by half globally.

5. What was the most fun project you ever participated in and why?

The most rewarding project was designing the secondary belt drive for an electric scooter. It required balancing very specific constraints, like a high reduction ratio and fixed pulley diameters. It was "fun" because it bridged the gap between textbook theory and a real-world machine you see on the streets every day. Seeing the math translate into a functional drivetrain that powers a vehicle is exactly why I chose this field.

My SAP TechEd Bangalore 2025 

 


My Experience at SAP TechEd Bangalore

Recently, I had the opportunity to attend SAP TechEd Bangalore, and it was an amazing learning experience for me. As a student interested in technology and innovation, being part of such a global tech event was truly inspiring. One of the most exciting parts of the event was exploring the Demo Pods, where SAP experts explained different technologies and solutions. It was great to see how SAP technologies like BTP are used to solve real-world business problems.

Key Takeaways & Highlights
While there was a lot to take in, these three themes defined the event for me:

The Power of Generative AI: The evolution of SAP Joule was front and center. It’s clear that AI is no longer a "plus-one" feature but the core engine driving developer productivity and business process automation.

A "Clean Core" is Non-Negotiable: There was a significant focus on the Clean Core Strategy. Learning about new extensibility patterns reinforced that staying close to the standard is the only way to ensure long-term agility and easier upgrades.

Driving Sustainability with Data: I was particularly inspired by the sessions on ESG reporting. Seeing how we can leverage BTP to turn sustainability goals into measurable data points is a game-changer for the modern enterprise.

The Best Part of SAP TechEd:

The Community  While the product announcements and technical roadmaps are what bring us to SAP TechEd Bangalore, it’s the people who make the experience unforgettable. One of my personal highlights was the chance to connect directly with fellow community members and SAP experts on the show floor. Whether we were discussing the nuances of BTP at the Demo Pods or simply sharing our learning journeys, these interactions are where the real "tech magic" happens.

 

SAP TechEd Bangalore – A Great Learning Experience with My College Team:

 


I had the wonderful opportunity to attend SAP TechEd Bangalore along with friends from my college. It was a great experience for all of us to be part of such a global technology event. Our college group explored different sessions, demo pods, and interacted with SAP experts. We also had the chance to connect with members of the SAP Community and learn about new technologies like SAP BTP, AI, and cloud innovations.

Learning journey:

 

The SAP Procurement Process provides a structured framework for managing the end-to-end lifecycle of material acquisition, ensuring seamless integration between departmental needs and vendor fulfillment. My training in this module covered the critical stages of the Procure-to-Pay (P2P) cycle, beginning with the strategic determination of requirements through Purchase Requisitions and moving into source selection via RFQs. By mastering the creation and monitoring of Purchase Orders, I gained a deep understanding of how standardized procurement documentation maintains data integrity and legal compliance within a corporate environment.

The latter phase of the workflow focuses on the logistical and financial reconciliation essential for operational accuracy. I am proficient in handling Goods Receipts—specifically understanding movement types and their impact on real-time inventory levels—and the vital Three-Way Match during Invoice Verification. This ensures that the Purchase Order, physical delivery, and vendor billing are perfectly aligned before payment. This expertise allows for the reduction of discrepancies, optimized stock management, and a streamlined flow of information across an organization’s supply chain.

Through this course, I have acquired the technical proficiency to manage the full SAP procurement cycle, focusing on inventory optimization, rigorous financial reconciliation, and the strategic management of supplier relationships.

Now, I would like to finish the post by asking you all a question which you're invited to answer in the comments section:

Which SAP modules offer the best career growth for beginners?

entry.title: Hello SAP Community! My name is Veera Venkata Satyanarayana, an Aspiring Mechanical Engineer Student
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community!

Moving to Bangalore was a major turning point for me. Seeing the rapid pace of technological evolution in this city sparked a realization: the future isn't just about hardware or software—it’s about how they work together.

Currently, I am a 3rd-year Mechanical Engineering student at Reva University. I’ve dedicated my time to mastering CAD (Catia V5) and simulation (Ansys), but I’ve also expanded my horizon into Python, full-stack web development, and AI. My goal is to apply these digital tools to solve real-world physical problems.

The Recently I Participated in a Hardware Hackathon at New Horizon college of Engineering,  Project: Autonomous Patient Request Receiver
Recently, my team and I participated in a hackathon where we built an Autonomous Patient Request Receiver. This was a deep dive into the practical side of robotics.


The Hardware: We used IR sensors for precision line-following and ultrasonic sensors for obstacle detection.

The Challenge: Our biggest hurdle was the "line-following debug" phase—ensuring the robot could handle tight turns and maintain stability under different lighting conditions.

Next Steps: While I’m currently experimenting with Agent Workflows (and learning from a few failed attempts!), I plan to document my progress and share a detailed technical breakdown in my next post.

Getting to Know Me

1. Which 5 things do you absolutely want to achieve in life?

  • Global Impact: Work on large-scale engineering projects in a tech-forward global environment.

  • Social Entrepreneurship: Build a sustainable business that positively impacts my local community.

  • Continuous Mastery: Reach a level of expertise where I can consult on complex "Physical AI" systems.

  • Innovation: Contribute to breakthroughs in automation and robotics that change how industries operate.

  • Quality of Life: Build a career that offers both a "comfort zone" for my family and high-level professional challenges.

2. What is the best lesson your parents taught you? Persistence and Untold Love. My parents taught me how to sustain myself through "hard flows." They showed me that when you get stuck, you don't stop—you build a new path. Beyond the lessons in critical thinking, they gave me an untold love—a foundation of selfless support that gives me the confidence to take risks and climb toward greater heights.

3. What do you imagine is the future of your topic? I believe the future lies in “Physical AI.” We have seen AI excel in handling data and text, but the next evolution is AI interacting seamlessly with the physical world through robotics and edge computing. I envision a future where platforms like SAP not only manage enterprise data but connect directly with autonomous systems on factory floors—enabling "dark warehouses" and real-time industrial decision-making.

4. Where do you see yourself in 5 years? I see myself in a leading technical role at a global MNC, bridging the gap between mechanical design and enterprise-level automation software.

SAP TechEd Bangalore Experience:

I would like to sincerely thank Reva University and the Mechanical Department for giving me the opportunity to attend SAP TechEd Bangalore.

Before attending, I had limited clarity about SAP. However, the event completely changed my perspective. I understood the evolution of SAP and its global impact across industries.

One of the most memorable moments was interacting with the SAP Community team and meeting Moshe Naveh. That interaction was a turning point for me.

I realized that SAP is much more than just ERP—it is a powerful ecosystem for automation, data management, and intelligent enterprise solutions.

Key Takeaways:

  1. Clear understanding of SAP’s role in digital transformation
  2. Awareness of SAP Learning resources and Certification paths
  3. Inspiration to explore SAP technologies as part of my career journey

now let’s hear from you in the comments section!

What skill do you believe is most important to stay relevant in the era of AI and automation?

entry.title: 🚀 CodeJam Curitiba: quando a comunidade mostra a sua força
entry.content:
Tem eventos que a gente organiza...E tem eventos que a gente vive...O CodeJam Curitiba foi vivido.

Desde cedo já dava para sentir que seria especial. A recepção com coffee, conversas animadas e aquela energia boa de quem estava ali para aprender de verdade. E, claro, a presença das lendas Thomas, Rich e Josh já elevava o nível da expectativa.

 

 

 

 

Às 9h iniciamos oficialmente. Nosso curador  @LuizSouza abriu o evento reforçando algo que eu acredito profundamente: comunidade não é sobre evento, é sobre conexão. É sobre troca real.

 

 

 

 

Na sequência, o professor Valter, representando a Escola Politécnica PUCPR, falou sobre a importância dessa aproximação entre universidade e mercado. E que privilégio poder realizar esse momento dentro de uma instituição que abriu as portas com tanto carinho.

 

 

 

 

Nosso patrocinador CHPRO, por meio do Marcelo Passaglia, trouxe algo que eu particularmente valorizo muito: trajetória. Histórias inspiram. Pessoas reais inspiram. E ver esse apoio genuíno ao ecossistema mostra que estamos no caminho certo.

 

 

 

 

E então, Josh. Energia pura. Daquelas que contagiam, que lembram por que a SAP Community é diferente.

 

 

 

 

 

🤖Mão na massa — e é aqui que a mágica acontece

Depois das falas, fomos para o hands-on. E que experiência.

Thomas e Rich trouxeram muitas novidades sobre IA e mostraram, na prática, o tamanho das possibilidades que temos nas mãos. Mas o que mais me marcou não foi só o conteúdo técnico.

 

 

 

Foi a proximidade.

Ver Thomas e Rich lado a lado com os participantes, sentados juntos, trocando, ensinando, aprendendo… isso é comunidade de verdade. Não é palco distante. É construção conjunta.

Na parte da tarde, o ambiente era de concentração, curiosidade e descoberta. Era bonito de ver.

🎁Encerramento com aquele sentimento de “quero mais”

Finalizamos com o tradicional sorteio, a foto oficial e mais um coffee para aquele networking que gera futuras conexões e oportunidades.

 

 

 

 

 

E sim… nossa mascote capivara marcou presença e roubou sorrisos por onde passou 🧡

Encerramos o dia com aquele sentimento coletivo de que algo importante começou.

💙Gratidão (daquelas sinceras)

Eu acredito que eventos assim só acontecem porque existem pessoas que acreditam junto.

Nossa gratidão:

À @antonia_zorn , da SAP Community, por confiar, escolher Curitiba e nos incluir na rota.

Ao Thomas e Rich, por compartilharem conhecimento com tanta generosidade.

Ao Josh, pela energia e por fortalecer continuamente essa comunidade global.

Ao nosso patrocinador CHPRO, que apoiou de coração aberto e tornou tudo possível.

À Escola Politécnica PUCPR, por nos receber como casa.

À Vitória e ao Vitor, que vieram da SAP São Leopoldo para apoiar os instrutores e fortalecer ainda mais a experiência do CodeJam.

Aos nossos curadores @SimoneAzevedo , @LuizSouza e @AdrianoCWB , que planejaram e executaram cada detalhe com dedicação.

 

 

 

 

 

E um agradecimento muito especial aos participantes.

Vocês se inscreveram, confiaram, compareceram e provaram algo muito importante:

Curitiba tem sede de conhecimento. Curitiba tem protagonismo. Curitiba tem força.

Vocês mostraram, na prática, o poder da nossa comunidade quando se une em torno de tecnologia, aprendizado e colaboração.

 

🎬Veja os melhores momentos

Os registros estão no vídeo Melhores momentos— mas a experiência, essa fica na memória.

Curitiba entrou no mapa do CodeJam.

E eu tenho certeza: esse é só o começo.

Que venham muitos outros.

Que venham mais conexões.

Que venham mais histórias para contar.

Nos vemos nos próximos eventos da SAP Community Curitiba.

Com carinho,

Simone Azevedo 💙

entry.title: Venue for SAP Inside Track 2026 Announced!
entry.content:

As one of Sydney’s most historic and character-filled venues, The Mint brings a unique sense of place to this year’s event.  Its heritage architecture and rich history create a memorable backdrop, while the striking modern auditorium and beautiful surrounds offer the perfect setting for a fresh, elevated SAP Inside Track experience.

Its CBD location on Macquarie Street is highly convenient for attendees, within walking distance of Martin Place, Town Hall and Wynyard.

More details coming soon, including registration, call for speakers, and sponsorship opportunities.

entry.title: SAP Integration Suite Licensing: A Practical Guide for Businesses and Architects
entry.content:
Introduction

Within the SAP ecosystem, developers benefit from extensive documentation and technical tutorials. However, business leaders, enterprise architects, procurement teams, and startups often struggle to find clear guidance on licensing, pricing, and commercial models—particularly for SAP Integration Suite.

This blog bridges that gap by offering a practical, business-focused overview of SAP Integration Suite licensing, along with real-world case studies that illustrate how organizations can select the most suitable commercial model.

Why Licensing Information Is Hard to Find

Unlike technical documentation, commercial details are not widely publicized. Key reasons include:

1. Tailored Pricing Models

SAP solutions are customised based on:

  • Organization size and industry
  • Integration volumes and complexity
  • Geographic presence
  • Compliance requirements
  • Existing SAP investments

2. Sales-Led Engagement Model

Licensing information is typically provided through:

  • SAP account executives
  • Authorized SAP partners
  • Commercial proposals and Statements of Work

3. Consumption-Based Cloud Economics

SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) follows a consumption-based model where costs depend on usage metrics such as:

  • Integration runtime capacity
  • Messages processed
  • API calls and events
  • Compute and memory utilization
  • Additional capabilities like B2B Integration and API Management

4. Rapid Evolution of SAP Offerings

SAP frequently enhances its cloud offerings, making static pricing references less reliable over time.

Overview of SAP Integration Suite Commercial Models

1. Free Tier

Best For: Developers, students, and innovation teams

Purpose: Learning, experimentation, and prototyping

Benefits:

  • No cost
  • Ideal for proofs of concept
  • Hands-on experience

Limitations:

  • Restricted resources
  • Not intended for production use

2. Pay-As-You-Go (PAYG)

Best For: Startups, SMEs, and organizations with unpredictable workloads

Benefits:

  • No upfront commitment
  • Pay only for what you consume
  • Rapid scalability and flexibility

Considerations:

  • Requires cost governance
  • Expenses increase with scale

3. Cloud Platform Enterprise Agreement (CPEA)

Best For: Large enterprises adopting multiple SAP BTP services

Benefits:

  • Prepaid cloud credits
  • Flexibility across SAP BTP services
  • Volume-based discounts
  • Centralized governance

Considerations:

  • Requires upfront financial commitment
  • Needs consumption planning

4. Subscription-Based Licensing

Best For: Organizations with stable and predictable workloads

Benefits:

  • Predictable costs
  • Simplified budgeting
  • Guaranteed capacity

Considerations:

  • Less flexibility compared to consumption models
  • Potential risk of underutilization

5. RISE with SAP

Best For: Organizations adopting SAP S/4HANA Cloud as part of digital transformation

Benefits:

  • Bundled services under a single contract
  • Simplified vendor management
  • Accelerated cloud adoption

Considerations:

  • Less granular pricing visibility
  • Best suited for SAP-centric landscapes

Decision Matrix: Choosing the Right Model

Business Scenario

Recommended Model

Learning and experimentation

Free Tier

Startups building their first integrations

Pay-As-You-Go

SMEs with growing integration needs

PAYG transitioning to CPEA

Enterprises adopting multiple BTP services

CPEA

Predictable, high-volume workloads

Subscription-Based Licensing

S/4HANA Cloud transformation initiatives

RISE with SAP

System integrators and consulting firms

PAYG for projects; CPEA for internal use

Key Cost Drivers for SAP Integration Suite

Cost Driver

Business Impact

Number of integrations

Influences runtime capacity

Message volume

Major contributor to consumption costs

API calls

Impacts API Management usage

B2B/EDI capabilities

Adds specialized costs

Number of environments (Dev, QA, Prod)

Increases consumption

Data transformation complexity

Affects processing requirements

High availability and scalability

Raises infrastructure costs

Security and compliance requirements

May increase operational overhead

Real-World Case Studies

Case Study 1: Startup Integrating SaaS Applications

Industry: HR Technology Startup

Challenge: Integrate a cloud-based HR platform with SAP S/4HANA for clients.

Solution: Pay-As-You-Go model.

Implementation:

  • Connected Salesforce, SAP S/4HANA, and SuccessFactors.
  • Leveraged API Management and Cloud Integration.
  • Scaled gradually based on customer demand.

Outcome:

  • Zero upfront investment.
  • Faster time-to-market.
  • Controlled operational expenses.

Why PAYG Was Ideal:

  • Unpredictable workloads.
  • Need for agility and cost efficiency.

Case Study 2: Manufacturing Enterprise Modernizing Legacy Systems

Industry: Global Manufacturing

Challenge: Replace point-to-point integrations across SAP ECC, Ariba, and third-party logistics providers.

Solution: Cloud Platform Enterprise Agreement (CPEA).

Implementation:

  • Standardized enterprise integrations using SAP Integration Suite.
  • Enabled EDI and API-based partner connectivity.
  • Utilized BTP credits across multiple services.

Outcome:

  • Reduced integration complexity.
  • Improved supply chain visibility.
  • Optimized costs through prepaid credits.

Why CPEA Was Ideal:

  • Large-scale, enterprise-wide adoption.
  • Need for flexibility across multiple BTP services.

Case Study 3: Retail Chain Managing Seasonal Demand

Industry: Retail and E-Commerce

Challenge: Handle fluctuating integration volumes during festive and seasonal peaks.

Solution: Pay-As-You-Go model.

Implementation:

  • Integrated SAP Commerce Cloud with logistics and payment gateways.
  • Scaled integrations during peak sales periods.

Outcome:

  • Cost optimization during off-peak seasons.
  • Elastic scalability during high demand.

Why PAYG Was Ideal:

  • Variable workloads and seasonal spikes.

Case Study 4: Banking Institution Requiring Predictable Costs

Industry: Banking and Financial Services

Challenge: Ensure secure, compliant, and stable integrations across core banking systems.

Solution: Subscription-Based Licensing.

Implementation:

  • Integrated SAP and non-SAP systems with strict governance.
  • Ensured regulatory compliance and high availability.

Outcome:

  • Predictable budgeting and financial planning.
  • Stable, mission-critical operations.

Why Subscription Was Ideal:

  • Consistent transaction volumes.
  • Strong compliance requirements.

Case Study 5: Enterprise Migrating to SAP S/4HANA Cloud

Industry: Professional Services

Challenge: Transform legacy systems while migrating to SAP S/4HANA Cloud.

Solution: RISE with SAP.

Implementation:

  • Leveraged pre-packaged integrations and clean-core principles.
  • Enabled seamless connectivity across cloud applications.

Outcome:

  • Simplified vendor and contract management.
  • Accelerated digital transformation.

Why RISE with SAP Was Ideal:

  • Unified approach to ERP modernization and integration.

A Typical Adoption Journey

Most organizations follow this path:

Free Tier → Pay-As-You-Go → CPEA or Subscription → RISE with SAP

This approach enables businesses to:

  • Validate use cases
  • Optimize investments
  • Scale efficiently
  • Reduce financial risks

Essential Questions to Ask SAP or Partners

Before selecting a commercial model, consider asking:

  1. What metrics determine Integration Suite costs?
  2. Which capabilities are included in the license?
  3. How are non-production environments billed?
  4. Are there volume discounts available?
  5. What governance tools exist for cost monitoring?
  6. Can the commercial model be converted later?
  7. What is the estimated total cost of ownership over three years?
  8. How does pricing scale as the organization grows?

Recommended Resources

  • SAP Discovery Center 
  • SAP BTP Pricing Calculator
  • SAP Help Portal
  • SAP Community Blogs and Q&A
  • SAP Learning Hub and openSAP
  • Authorized SAP Partners and Account Executives

Key Takeaways

  • Pay-As-You-Go is a licensing model—not a license-free option.
  • CPEA provides flexibility and cost optimization for enterprises.
  • Subscription models offer predictable costs for stable workloads.
  • RISE with SAP simplifies ERP-led transformations.
  • Architects must align technical architecture with commercial strategy.
  • Understanding licensing early prevents costly redesigns later.

Conclusion

While developers benefit from extensive technical guidance, businesses and architects often lack clarity around licensing and pricing. By understanding SAP Integration Suite’s commercial models, organizations can make informed decisions that align technology investments with business goals.

Selecting the right licensing model ensures scalability, cost efficiency, and long-term success in the cloud integration journey.

Call to Action

Have you implemented SAP Integration Suite using PAYG, CPEA, Subscription, or RISE with SAP? Share your experiences and insights in the comments to help the SAP Community learn and grow together.

Thank you for reading!

Connect in LinkedIn : Naveen Kumar 

Disclaimer:
This article reflects personal insights and is intended for educational and informational purposes only. It does not represent the official position of SAP. Pricing, licensing, and commercial models are subject to change and may vary by region and customer agreement. For the most accurate and up-to-date information, please consult SAP directly or an authorized SAP partner. SAP and other SAP products mentioned herein are trademarks or registered trademarks of SAP SE in Germany and other countries.

entry.title: A memorable SAP Community gathering at the SAP Inside Track Campinas 2026
entry.content:
The SAP Inside Track Campinas 2026, the SIT edition number 60th in Brazil, was truly a remarkable event bringing together an impressive lineup of speakers and community members. It marked a special moment for the SAP ecosystem in Brazil, especially with the first visit of SAP Developer Advocates to the country. 

We had the honor of welcoming @noravonthenen @thomas_jung @RichHeilman @Vitaliy-R and SAP Community managers @antonia_zorn @josh_bentley, although Antonia is based in Brazil for some time, originally comes from Germany. Their presence, along with the broader SAP Community in Brazil, made this event in Campinas even more meaningful.

This visit was part of an extensive SAP CodeJam Roadshow across Brazil. For the first time, the country hosted more than 17 CodeJam sessions, hands-on training events focused on SAP development. It was an impressive milestone, and you can check the full events agenda in Brazil through this link (SIT + Codejam + SAP Meet ups).

The event itself took place at FACAMP, a beautiful university campus surrounded by green areas and open spaces. The day started with a warm welcome coffee break, followed by a full agenda that included lunch and additional networking breaks.

The opening session featured Josh and Antonia Zorn the SAP Community, followed by Thomas Jung and Rich, who spoke about ABAP AI topics that have been gaining strong traction in recent CodeJams. The agenda continued with several high-quality sessions from speakers like Vitaliy, Nora, @Secco , Leandro Dante, @MRobalinho among others. I’ve included links below so you can watch all sessions on YouTube.

 

Session Title Speaker(s)

ABAP AI — Artificial Intelligence in ABAP DevelopmentSAP Community managers @antonia_zorn @josh_bentley
SAP Developer Advocates @thomas_jung @RichHeilman
Tax Reform — 2026 RoadmapRoberto Paiva
GenAI — A Practical View of Generative AI in the SAP Ecosystem@noravonthenen
AI in SAP HANA Cloud@Vitaliy-R
Automatic PIX Implementation@MRobalinho 
AI, Geopolitics, and the IT Bubble — The SAP Architecture Market OutlookMarcelo Figueiredo Cabrera
From Data to Decisions — Accelerating Results with SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC)Renata Cristina
Transformation Focused on Business Value GenerationRodrigo Cordeiro
SAP BTP in Practice — Integrating Solutions and Connecting SAP ProductsDiego Yegros
SAP BTP Multitenancy in Practice — From Add-On to the FutureJoão Luiz Martins
GROW Retail Fashion — Architecture, Scalability, and Changes in S/4HANA Cloud (Public Edition)Diego Bortolucci
LLM, Joule, and RPT-1 — The New AI Stack for Structured Data in SAPLeandro Dante
AI for Functional Consultants — From Basic GPT to Prototyping@Secco 
Giveaways & Raffles — Closing SessionOrganization

One of the highlights of the event was its relaxed and friendly atmosphere. Attendees felt comfortable engaging with speakers, taking photos, and networking throughout the day. Unlike many events where people attend only a few sessions, most participants stayed until the very end, clear evidence of the quality and engagement of the content.

For me personally, it was also a special experience. I had the opportunity to contribute as a volunteer, supporting the live streaming of the event. While that meant I couldn’t take as many photos as I would have liked, it was incredibly rewarding to reconnect with friends and meet so many great professionals from the SAP community. 

Campinas continues to be a key hub for SAP events, attracting not only local participants but also many attendees from São Paulo. Alongside other cities like Ribeirão Preto and São José dos Campos, it plays an important role in strengthening the SAP ecosystem in the region.

 If you are an SAP professional, I strongly recommend keeping an eye on upcoming SAP Inside Track events in your area. Follow the SAP Community São Paulo group on LinkedIn and stay connected, you don’t want to miss these opportunities for learning, networking, and reconnecting with amazing people in the SAP world.

Special thanks to @RobertaFernande and the organizing team for making this event possible.

Feel free to share your own photos and experiences, or check out the SAP Inside Track LinkedIn page for more highlights from the event. The level of community engagement and social media activity around this event was truly outstanding.

It was a pleasure to be part of SAP Inside Track Campinas 2026. Looking forward to seeing you all again at future events.

entry.title: Introduction- Abhishek
entry.content:
Hi everyone,
 
I’ve been following SAP Community content for a while, and finally decided to become more active here.
 
I currently work as a Solutions Architect focusing on SAP BTP and S/4HANA transformation programs. Most of my work revolves around designing extension architectures, managing multi-subaccount landscapes, and setting up governance models.
 
I’m particularly interested in topics like:
  • Subaccount strategy and landscape design
  • Identity and access management across BTP
  • Transport and deployment governance (CTMS, DevOps patterns)
  • AI use cases
  • Clean-core aligned extension approaches
I’m here to learn from others’ experiences and also share some of the patterns and lessons I’ve seen in production environments.
 
Looking forward to connecting and contributing.

entry.title: SAP Test Automation PM from Korea — Excited to Join the Community

I'm Arum, a Product Manager at LG CNS in Seoul, South Korea, working on SAP test automation.
